content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

September 17, 1991

Ernest R. Hawkins  
Registrar of Voters  
County of Sacramento  
Voter Registration & Elections  
3700 Branch Center Road  
Sacramento, CA 95827

RE: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-91-389

Dear Mr. Hawkins:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Is an optical disk storage system an acceptable means of retention for campaign disclosure statements filed with the Registrar's office?

CONCLUSION

After an original document has been on file for at least two years, a filing officer may utilize any space saving device that is capable of retaining documents indefinitely.

FACTS

Sacramento County Voter Registration and Elections has installed an Electronic Imaging System for the storage and retrieval of campaign disclosure documents. Campaign disclosure statements are currently being scanned for retention purposes.

ANALYSIS

The Act provides that once a document has been on file for two years, the document may be placed on microfilm or some other space-saving device and the original paper document may be destroyed. Specifically, Section 81009(g) states:

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

After an original report or statement or a copy has been on file for at least two years, the officer with whom it is filed may comply with this section by retaining a copy on microfilm or other space-saving materials available for public inspection...

The Act does not provide the filing officer with guidance as to the means of retaining documents, e.g., film versus optical disk. The filing officer must ensure that the transmittal from paper document to another means of retention will result in the public's access to the campaign statements over an indefinite period of time.

Please contact me at 916/322-5662 if I may be of further assistance.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant
